prompt: A global ITSM program must deploy Incident, Problem, and Change processes across four regions over twelve months. Which rollout strategy reduces operational risk?
choices: Phase by region and process with pilot validation, defined cutover milestones, and rollback plans before scaling
Enable every ITSM application globally on day one without user acceptance testing
Deploy only in production while disabling sub-production validation environments
Skip CAB design because emergency changes will cover all early defects
explanation: Phased ITSM rollouts validate process design, integrations, and training in pilots before expanding to additional regions and functions.

prompt: Legacy ticketing data and CMDB exports must load before decommissioning the previous tool. Which migration strategy should the implementation team use?
choices: Cleanse and map historical incidents, problems, changes, and CIs with reconciliation validation before cutover
Import all closed tickets with duplicate callers and fix data only after go-live
Ask agents to re-create five years of records manually in ServiceNow
Disable identification rules during migration and never reconcile duplicates
explanation: ITSM migrations require field mapping, data cleansing, and reconciliation so operational processes start with trustworthy records.

prompt: Executive sponsors want measurable success six months after ITSM go-live. Which KPI set should the program define upfront?
choices: Incident resolution time, change success rate, problem recurrence reduction, and catalog fulfillment SLA adherence
Count of UI policies created on unrelated platform tables
Number of discovery probes installed without linking to service outcomes
Total catalog items visible regardless of fulfillment performance
explanation: ITSM KPIs align process performance with business outcomes across incident, change, problem, and request fulfillment domains.

prompt: Agents revert to email and spreadsheets two weeks after launch. Which governance and adoption approach should the ITSM lead implement?
choices: Define process owners, role-based training, communication plans, and enforcement of standard workflows in ServiceNow
Remove all approvals so changes and incidents close faster regardless of policy
Disable the service catalog to force users back to unstructured email only
Grant every user admin access to reduce permission-related frustration
explanation: Sustainable ITSM adoption combines clear process governance, stakeholder communications, and training tied to defined roles and workflows.
